question_text: A tennis ball with a mass of 0.06 kg falls to the ground. The gravitational field strength is 10 N/kg. Calculate the weight of the ball.
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula: weight = mass x gravitational field strength. So weight = 0.06 kg x 10 N/kg = 0.6 N. Weight is measured in newtons and is a force caused by gravity.
